prompt: Write the opening paragraph of a children's book about a shy octopus who wants to make friends.
response: Oliver Octopus lived at the very bottom of Rainbow Reef, behind a curtain of soft pink seaweed that swayed like sleepy dancers. He had eight twisty arms, three secret hiding holes, and one big worry: everyone else was playing splash-tag above the coral towers, but Oliver was too shy to wave even a single tentacle. Every time a giggling sea turtle or a twirling shrimp drifted by, Oliver blushed the color of a ripe peach and tucked himself into the tiniest swirl of sand, wishing he could shout, “Wait for me!”—but the words always bubbled away before they reached his lips.
